danger_signals.visual_form: Lava river in Scorpion's Lair/Hell stage; hazardous environmental features
danger_signals.meaning: Ultimate danger zone—environment itself is weaponized; uppercuts can send opponent into lava

danger_signals.visual_form: Opponent visually trapped against stage boundary
danger_signals.meaning: Vulnerability state; limited escape routes increase damage intake risk

danger_signals.visual_form: Decapitation and other extreme violence during character defeat sequences
danger_signals.meaning: Complete combat dominance; opponent is incapacitated and defeated

safety_signals.visual_form: Character positioned at center or neutral ground distance from opponent
safety_signals.meaning: Mobility and escape routes available; reduced immediate threat

safety_signals.visual_form: Character performing evasive backward flip away from opponent
safety_signals.meaning: Defensive repositioning; temporary reprieve from direct pressure

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Liu Kang, Kitana, Jade, Scorpion (returning characters)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Desert stages (Jade's Desert); waterfront and river kombat arenas suggest realm-specific territories

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Shao Kahn (implied antagonist through 'Lost Treasures' and tournament structure)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Kahn's Kave/Cavern; Scorpion's Lair/Hell stages; dark portal backgrounds suggest Outworld domain spaces

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Reptile, Scorpion, Kitana, Jade (ninja character group)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Noob's Dorfen (Balcony stage); emphasizes hidden/shadow spaces

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Character select screens with full-body portraits; stage backgrounds change per match
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: 8-player tournament mode; 2-on-2 endurance matches indicate escalating stakes and multi-round survival

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Portal backgrounds (Blue Portal/Lost stage); visual blend of multiple stage aesthetics (Pit 3 bridge + Pit II mountains)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Stage Fatalities allow uppercuts to knock opponents between layered environments (Scorpion's Lair to Kahn's Kave transition)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Cavern stages with dark palette; Shao Kahn's Lost Treasures system frames progression as Kahn-controlled rewards
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Tournament completion unlocks Kahn-associated cutscenes and optional fights

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Lava rivers (Scorpion's Lair); sand traps (Jade's Desert with Cyrax stuck waist-deep); water stages (River Kombat/Waterfront)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Stage Fatalities trigger when specific conditions met (uppercuts in lava); environmental interaction affects combat strategy

environmental_storytelling.example: Cyrax character visible stuck in sand in Jade's Desert background; references his MK3 story ending and implies failed invasion/entrapment narrative
Scorpion's Lair named 'Hell'—visual and naming convention establishes underworld dominion; Jade's Desert and River Kombat suggest Earthrealm natural territories
Blue Portal/Lost stage combines visual elements from multiple prior game stages—communicates dimensional overlap, fracture, or convergence of realms
Original red portal changed to blue in UMK3—subtle visual world-state change implies altered dimensional properties or magical transformation
Lava in Hell stage makes it visually distinct from other realms; immediate visual danger communicates this is Outworld/hostile domain

entity_behavioral_tells.state: In range of player walking back-and-forth
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Mimics player walking pattern for entire round; never attacks—indicates AI passivity or confusion state
Does not stumble back from repeated punches; becomes easily defeated—vulnerability state with reduced evasion capability

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Player backflipping away with jump kick performed
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Always throws projectile in response—predictable reaction creates exploitable opening for player combo setup
Executes extreme violence (decapitation demonstrated); visually signals complete dominance and opponent elimination

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Chain combo activation via jump punch/kick
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Increased combo opportunity window opens; character gains offensive momentum state

notes: UMK3 uses stage hazards, environmental transitions, and character positioning as primary visual danger/safety indicators. Faction identity is communicated through character roster grouping (ninja clan vs. warriors) and stage environment association (desert, cavern, waterfront) rather than explicit color or sigil coding. World-state is conveyed through tournament progression structure, portal/dimensional visuals, and environmental narrative details (background dioramas like Cyrax in sand). The game lacks explicit political faction UI markers but implies Shao Kahn's antagonistic dominion through stage naming and treasure reward system framing.
